content.thinking: Let me start by analyzing the codebase to understand the project structure, providers, CSS files, queries, and other runtime environment details.

input.prompt:
Thoroughly analyze this React/TypeScript frontend codebase. I need to understand the complete runtime environment to set up Storybook properly.

Working directory: /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-11T17-41-16Z-cb016168/project/frontend

Please explore:
1. src/main.tsx - entry point, providers, CSS imports
2. src/App.tsx - provider tree, routing
3. All context/provider files
4. All hooks that fetch data or read browser state (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies)
5. Any components using portals (createPortal, modal, dialog, drawer, toast)
6. CSS files that are globally imported
7. The package.json for dependencies
8. .storybook/ folder if it exists
9. Any existing test files for patterns
10. Pages and major components (read their actual contents)

I need to know:
- Which providers wrap the app
- Which CSS files are imported globally
- Which API endpoints are fetched
- What localStorage/sessionStorage keys are read during render
- Any portal roots (div#portal-root, div#modal-root, etc.)
- The full list of components available for stories

Please read the actual file contents, not just list them. Read main.tsx, App.tsx, all providers/contexts, all hooks, and at least 10-15 component files to understand the patterns.

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive report based on all the information I've gathered: ## COMPREHENSIVE RUNTIME ENVIRONMENT ANALYSIS Based on my thorough exploration of the React/TypeScript frontend codebase, here's the complete runtime environment analysis for setting up Storybook: --- ### 1. PROVIDER TREE & DEPENDENCY INJECTION **Entry Point**: `/src/main.tsx` ``` Root ├── React.StrictMode └── LikedArticlesProvider (Context-based) └── App ``` **Single Provider**: `LikedArticlesProvider` wraps the entire app tree to manage liked articles state and expose the `useLikedArticles` hook. --- ### 2. CSS IMPORTS & GLOBAL STYLES **Global CSS Files Imported**: - `/src/index.css` - Main global stylesheet (imported in main.tsx) - Uses Tailwind CSS with `@import "tailwindcss"` - Custom scrollbar hiding classes (.hide-scroll) - html/body overflow behavioral rules **Component-Level CSS**: - `/src/styles/Article.css` - Article component styles - `/src/assets/heartAnimation.css` - Heart animation for likes (imported in LikedArticlesContext) - `/src/stories/*.css` - Storybook example component styles (button.css, header.css, page.css) **CSS Framework**: Tailwind CSS v4.0.3 with @tailwindcss/postcss and @tailwindcss/vite --- ### 3. CONTEXT/PROVIDER DETAILS **LikedArticlesContext** (`/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx`): - **Purpose**: Manages user's liked articles with persistence - **localStorage Key**: `"likedArticles"` - stores serialized array of WikiArticle objects - **Exports**: `useLikedArticles()` hook with shape: ```typescript { likedArticles: WikiArticle[], toggleLike: (article: WikiArticle) => void, isLiked: (pageid: number) => boolean } ``` - **Side Effects**: Renders animated heart on like (using heart-animation CSS class) - **Initialization**: Reads from localStorage on mount, defaults to empty array --- ### 4. DATA FETCHING HOOKS & API CALLS **useWikiArticles** (`/src/hooks/useWikiArticles.ts`): - **API Endpoint**: Dynamic per language from `currentLanguage.api` (Wikipedia API endpoints) - **Base URLs**: Multiple language-specific Wikipedia APIs (en, ar, bn, ca, de, es, etc. - 50+ languages) - **Fetch Pattern**: ``` GET /w/api.php?action=query&generator=random&prop=extracts|info|pageimages ``` - **Query Parameters**: - `action=query` - `generator=random` (random articles) - `grnnamespace=0` (mainspace only) - `prop=extracts|info|pageimages` (text, metadata, thumbnails) - `exintro=1` (intro only) - `exlimit=max` & `exsentences=5` (5 sentence extracts) - `explaintext=1` (plain text) - `piprop=thumbnail&pithumbsize=800` (thumbnails) - `origin=*` (CORS) - **Data Shape Returned**: ```typescript WikiArticle { title: string, displaytitle: string, extract: string, pageid: number, url: string, thumbnail: { source: string, width: number, height: number } } ``` - **Buffering**: Fetches in batches of 20 articles with preloading of images - **State**: `articles`, `loading`, `buffer` **useLocalization** (`/src/hooks/useLocalization.ts`): - **localStorage Key**: `"lang"` - stores language ID (default: English) - **Returns**: `currentLanguage` (Language object) and `setLanguage` function - **Side Effect**: `window.location.reload()` on language change - **Languages**: 50+ languages with Wikipedia API URLs and flag images --- ### 5. localStorage & SessionStorage KEYS Read during render/initialization: - `"likedArticles"` - Array of liked WikiArticle objects (LikedArticlesProvider) - `"lang"` - Language ID string (useLocalization, default: "en") --- ### 6. PORTAL ROOTS & SPECIAL DOM ELEMENTS **No createPortal usage detected in app code.** **Main Root Element**: `
` in index.html **No modal/dialog/toast portal roots** - The app uses inline conditional rendering (not React Portals) for: - About modal (inline in App.tsx) - Likes sidebar (inline in App.tsx) - Heart animation (renders from LikedArticlesProvider) --- ### 7. COMPLETE COMPONENT INVENTORY **Core App Components**: 1. `App` (`/src/App.tsx`) - Main entry point, manages UI state 2. `WikiCard` (`/src/components/WikiCard.tsx`) - Single article card (full-screen card UI) 3. `LanguageSelector` (`/src/components/LanguageSelector.tsx`) - Language dropdown 4. `ArticleList` (`/src/components/ArticleList.tsx`) - List wrapper for articles 5. `Article` (`/src/components/Article.tsx`) - Generic article component **Storybook Example Components**: 1. `Button` (`/src/stories/Button.tsx`) - Reusable button component 2. `Header` (`/src/stories/Header.tsx`) - Header with user state 3. `Page` (`/src/stories/Page.tsx`) - Full page example **Types**: - `WikiArticle` interface (in WikiCard.tsx) - `ArticleProps` type (`/src/types/ArticleProps.ts`) - `Language` configuration (in languages.ts) --- ### 8. STORYBOOK CONFIGURATION **Location**: `/.storybook/` **main.ts** (`/.storybook/main.ts`): ```typescript { stories: ['../src/**/*.mdx', '../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)', './eval-support/*.mdx'], addons: [ '@chromatic-com/storybook', '@storybook/addon-vitest', '@storybook/addon-a11y', '@storybook/addon-docs', '@storybook/addon-onboarding' ], framework: '@storybook/react-vite' } ``` **preview.tsx** (`/.storybook/preview.tsx`): ```typescript { parameters: { controls: { matchers: { color: /(background|color)$/i, date: /Date$/i } }, a11y: { test: 'todo' } } } ``` **Current Stories**: - Button (Primary, Secondary, Large, Small variants) - Header (LoggedIn, LoggedOut states) - Page (LoggedOut, LoggedIn with interaction testing) --- ### 9. PACKAGE.json DEPENDENCIES **Core React**: - react ^18.3.1 - react-dom ^18.3.1 **UI & Styling**: - tailwindcss ^4.0.3 - @tailwindcss/postcss ^4.0.3 - @tailwindcss/vite ^4.0.3 - lucide-react ^0.474.0 (icons) **Build & Dev**: - vite ^6.0.5 - @vitejs/plugin-react ^4.3.4 - typescript ~5.6.2 - vite-plugin-pwa ^0.21.1 (PWA support) **Storybook** (pre-release build): - storybook 0.0.0-pr-34421-sha-8babe7bc - @storybook/react-vite (same version) - @storybook/addon-vitest (same version) - @storybook/addon-a11y (same version) - @storybook/addon-docs (same version) - @storybook/addon-onboarding (same version) - @chromatic-com/storybook ^5.1.1 **Testing**: - vitest ^4.1.2 - @vitest/browser-playwright ^4.1.2 - @vitest/coverage-v8 ^4.1.2 - playwright ^1.58.2 **Analytics**: - @vercel/analytics ^1.4.1 --- ### 10. VITE CONFIGURATION DETAILS **vite.config.ts**: - React plugin enabled - Tailwind CSS plugin enabled - PWA plugin with auto-update registration - Vitest integration with Storybook addon-vitest - Playwright browser testing configured - Special Playwright config for mobile screenshots (iPhone 17 userAgent, 393x852 viewport, 2x scale) --- ### 11. KEY RUNTIME CHARACTERISTICS **Browser State Access**: - `navigator.share()` - for native share (WikiCard) - `navigator.clipboard` - for fallback link copying - `IntersectionObserver` - for infinite scroll pagination (App.tsx) - `localStorage` - for persistence (2 keys: lang, likedArticles) **Double-Click Handler**: - WikiCard responds to double-click to toggle like (like TikTok) **Image Preloading**: - All fetched article images preloaded before adding to display list **Analytics**: - Vercel Analytics component renders in App.tsx --- ### 12. EXISTING STORYBOOK STORIES & PATTERNS **Story Files**: - `/src/stories/Button.stories.ts` - Meta + 4 variants (Primary, Secondary, Large, Small) - `/src/stories/Header.stories.ts` - Meta + 2 states (LoggedIn, LoggedOut) - `/src/stories/Page.stories.ts` - Meta + 2 states + interaction test (play function) **Testing Pattern**: Using `expect`, `userEvent`, `within` from 'storybook/test' **Addon Configuration**: Vitest addon enabled for play function testing, A11y addon for accessibility tests --- ### CRITICAL SETUP NOTES FOR STORYBOOK 1. **Provider Wrapping**: Stories using `useLikedArticles` or `useLocalization` MUST wrap components with `LikedArticlesProvider` 2. **API Calls**: `useWikiArticles` makes real Wikipedia API calls - may want to mock in Storybook stories 3. **localStorage**: Both hooks read localStorage on mount - need to mock or reset between stories 4. **Window.reload**: `setLanguage` calls `window.location.reload()` - avoid in Storybook or mock 5. **No Portals**: App uses inline rendering, so no special portal setup needed 6. **Tailwind**: Global Tailwind v4 styles imported - should work in preview.tsx 7. **Mobile-First**: App is mobile-optimized with specific viewport handling in vite.config 8. **PWA Ready**: Service worker registration in place (handled by vite-plugin-pwa) --- **File Paths Summary**: - Entry: `/src/main.tsx` - App Root: `/src/App.tsx` - Providers: `/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx` - Hooks: `/src/hooks/useWikiArticles.ts`, `/src/hooks/useLocalization.ts` - Components: `/src/components/WikiCard.tsx`, `/src/components/LanguageSelector.tsx`, `/src/components/ArticleList.tsx`, `/src/components/Article.tsx` - Types: `/src/types/ArticleProps.ts` - Config: `/src/languages.ts` - CSS: `/src/index.css`, `/src/styles/Article.css`, `/src/assets/heartAnimation.css` - Storybook: `/.storybook/main.ts`, `/.storybook/preview.tsx`

5→import { LanguageSelector } from "./components/LanguageSelector"; 6→import { useLikedArticles } from "./contexts/LikedArticlesContext"; 7→import { useWikiArticles } from "./hooks/useWikiArticles"; 8→ 9→function App() { 10→ const [showAbout, setShowAbout] = useState(false); 11→ const [showLikes, setShowLikes] = useState(false); 12→ const { articles, loading, fetchArticles } = useWikiArticles(); 13→ const { likedArticles, toggleLike } = useLikedArticles(); 14→ const observerTarget = useRef(null); 15→ const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(""); 16→ 17→ const handleObserver = useCallback( 18→ (entries: IntersectionObserverEntry[]) => { 19→ const [target] = entries; 20→ if (target.isIntersecting && !loading) { 21→ fetchArticles(); 22→ } 23→ }, 24→ [loading, fetchArticles] 25→ ); 26→ 27→ useEffect(() => { 28→ const observer = new IntersectionObserver(handleObserver, { 29→ threshold: 0.1, 30→ rootMargin: "100px", 31→ }); 32→ 33→ if (observerTarget.current) { 34→ observer.observe(observerTarget.current); 35→ } 36→ 37→ return () => observer.disconnect(); 38→ }, [handleObserver]); 39→ 40→ useEffect(() => { 41→ fetchArticles(); 42→ }, []); 43→ 44→ const filteredLikedArticles = likedArticles.filter( 45→ (article) => 46→ article.title.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase()) || 47→ article.extract.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase()) 48→ ); 49→ 50→ const handleExport = () => { 51→ const simplifiedArticles = likedArticles.map((article) => ({ 52→ title: article.title, 53→ url: article.url, 54→ extract: article.extract, 55→ thumbnail: article.thumbnail?.source || null, 56→ })); 57→ 58→ const dataStr = JSON.stringify(simplifiedArticles, null, 2); 59→ const dataUri = 60→ "data:application/json;charset=utf-8," + encodeURIComponent(dataStr); 61→ 62→ const exportFileDefaultName = `wikitok-favorites-${new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0] 63→ }.json`; content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me plan what I need to do:

1. **Update `.storybook/preview.tsx`** to:
   - Import `../src/index.css`
   - Wrap stories with `LikedArticlesProvider`
   - Seed `localStorage.setItem("lang", "en")` for localization
   - Set up MSW for Wikipedia API calls

2. **Set up MSW** since the app makes API calls to Wikipedia:
   - Install `msw` and `msw-storybook-addon`
   - Initialize MSW in the public directory and configure it in main.ts
   - Create mock handlers for Wikipedia API responses in `.storybook/msw-handlers.ts`

3. **Write stories** for the key components: `WikiCard`, `LanguageSelector`, `ArticleList`, `Article`, and the main App with MSW data. I'm thinking through which components are most important to test—`WikiCard` is definitely the primary target since it's the core card component.

For `WikiCard`, I should cover the default state with a thumbnail, a variant without one, and a liked state. `LanguageSelector` needs both closed and open dropdown states. `ArticleList` should show cases with articles, empty state, and with a selection handler. `Article` needs variants with and without images, plus long content. The App story will need MSW to mock the Wikipedia API and show the loading and loaded states.

I need to add MSW to the dependencies first—let me check the lockfile to see which package manager is being used.

Actually, thinking about this more carefully: `WikiCard` and `ArticleList` don't fetch data directly since they receive article data as props, so those stories won't need MSW. Only the App story will need it since that's where the Wikipedia API call happens.

My plan is to set up the preview with the necessary providers and styles, then write stories for the simpler components first with static data, and handle MSW setup for the App story. Let me check what's in the lockfile to see which package manager is being used, then I'll need to import the CSS file into the preview configuration.

The project uses Bun as the package manager. Let me install MSW and msw-storybook-addon.

Now let me plan all the work:

1. Install MSW packages
2. Run `npx msw init ./public --save`
3. Update `.storybook/main.ts` to add `staticDirs`
4. Update `.storybook/preview.tsx` with LikedArticlesProvider, CSS, MSW setup
5. Create `.storybook/msw-handlers.ts` with Wikipedia API mock


6. Write stories for WikiCard, LanguageSelector, ArticleList, Article, and App

Let me start by setting up MSW and the preview. For the Wikipedia API mock, I need to handle the endpoint at `https://en.wikipedia.org/w/api.php` and return a response with the query structure containing pages with article metadata like pageid, title, and extract.

For MSW, I'll intercept the Wikipedia API calls with a handler. My setup plan is to install MSW and the Storybook addon, initialize MSW in the public directory, update the Storybook configuration files, create the mock handlers, and then write the story files for each component.

Actually, let me verify something about the setup first.

The Storybook version being used is a pre-release canary build, so I need to check if `msw-storybook-addon` will work properly with it. The instructions say to use it though, so I'll proceed with that approach.

Looking at which components actually need mocking, I realize that `WikiCard`, `Article`, and `ArticleList` all accept their data as props, so they don't need MSW at all. Only the `App` component and the `useWikiArticles` hook make actual API calls, so those are the only ones that require mocking. I can still write comprehensive stories for the other components by passing in test data directly.

For the `App` component, I'll need to set up MSW, but first I should check if the baseline Storybook setup already has any MSW configuration or a service worker in place.

content.thinking:
The issue with `WithArticlesLoaded` is that it's waiting for `getByText(/Albert Einstein/)` but that text appears in multiple elements (both the heading and the paragraph text). Also, the test seems to find the wrong story - it's showing "No Preview" which suggests the canvas is using the iframe document context from Storybook, not the actual story component.

Wait, but we can see from the stdout that the articles are being fetched and loaded. The issue is that `canvas.getByText(/Albert Einstein/)` fails because it matches multiple elements.

Let me fix the story to use a more specific query. I'll use `getAllByText` or use `getByRole('heading', ...)`.
 
The "No Preview" message in the canvas is confusing since the logs show articles are actually being loaded and displayed - the test is probably querying the wrong document context or the App isn't rendering properly in the Storybook canvas.

The real issue is likely that the snap scroll layout pushes article cards outside the visible viewport, so they exist in the DOM but aren't visible to the test. I should use `waitFor` to check for the article heading specifically, or just verify the WikiCard element exists rather than checking visibility.

Looking at the test code, `getByText(/Albert Einstein/)` might be matching multiple elements since the name appears in both the heading and the article extract, which could be causing the assertion to fail unexpectedly. The issue is likely that the article is rendering off-screen due to the scrollable container layout, so the visibility check fails even though the element exists in the DOM. I should switch to querying for the heading without the visibility requirement, or use `getAllByRole` to handle potential duplicates and check with `toBeInTheDocument()` instead.

Actually, I'm realizing the real problem: the articles are being duplicated because the initial fetch adds 3 articles, then the buffer fetch immediately adds the same 3 articles again, resulting in 6 total with duplicate keys. When I query for "Albert Einstein", it finds two matching elements. I should use `getAllByRole('heading', { name: /Albert Einstein/i })` and verify at least one is present in the document.

The TypeScript errors are:
1. Story files have required `args` properties - when using `render` instead of `args`, the components have required props that TypeScript expects to be in `args`. I need to add `args: {}` to the meta or use a different approach.

The issue is that `Article`, `ArticleList`, and `WikiCard` all have required props (`title`, `content`, `article`, `articles`), and TypeScript is complaining because the story type requires `args` to be defined.

The fix is to add an `args` property to the meta or to the individual stories. Since we're using `render` functions that provide all props directly, we should add `args: {}` to the meta to tell TypeScript we're not relying on args binding.


Actually, I should reconsider the approach here. The real solution is to either define a default `render` function at the meta level that handles the required props, or to relax the strict typing by not using `satisfies Meta<typeof Component>` so TypeScript doesn't enforce the args requirement. Setting default args at the meta level with empty strings for required props like title and content satisfies TypeScript's type constraints, while still allowing individual stories to override everything through render functions. I'll add these minimal defaults to the meta for any stories with required props.

content.text:
All 17 tests pass across 5 story files. Here's a summary of everything that was set up:

## Summary

### Preview setup (`.storybook/preview.tsx`)
- Imports `../src/index.css` (Tailwind v4 global styles)
- Wraps all stories with `LikedArticlesProvider` (the only app provider)
- Seeds `localStorage.setItem('lang', 'en')` before each story (read by `useLocalization`)
- Configures MSW with `initialize` + `mswLoader` for Wikipedia API mocking

### MSW setup
- Installed `msw` + `msw-storybook-addon`
- Ran `msw init ./public` to generate the service worker
- Added `staticDirs: ['../public']` to `main.ts`
- Created `.storybook/msw-handlers.ts` with a global Wikipedia API mock returning 3 realistic articles (Einstein, Curie, Everest)

### Stories written (17 tests total)

| File | Stories |
|------|---------|
| `src/components/Article.stories.tsx` | Default, WithImage, LongContent |
| `src/components/ArticleList.stories.tsx` | Default, Empty, WithSelectionHandler |
| `src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx` | Default, WithExtract, NoThumbnail, Liked |
| `src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx` | Default, DropdownOpen, DropdownShowsLanguages |
| `src/App.stories.tsx` | Default, AboutModalOpen, LikesModalOpen, WithArticlesLoaded |

Every story has a meaningful `play` function with assertions. The `App` stories exercise the full provider tree, MSW-mocked Wikipedia API, and modal interactions.

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive report based on all the information I've gathered: ## COMPREHENSIVE RUNTIME ENVIRONMENT ANALYSIS Based on my thorough exploration of the React/TypeScript frontend codebase, here's the complete runtime environment analysis for setting up Storybook: --- ### 1. PROVIDER TREE & DEPENDENCY INJECTION **Entry Point**: `/src/main.tsx` ``` Root ├── React.StrictMode └── LikedArticlesProvider (Context-based) └── App ``` **Single Provider**: `LikedArticlesProvider` wraps the entire app tree to manage liked articles state and expose the `useLikedArticles` hook. --- ### 2. CSS IMPORTS & GLOBAL STYLES **Global CSS Files Imported**: - `/src/index.css` - Main global stylesheet (imported in main.tsx) - Uses Tailwind CSS with `@import "tailwindcss"` - Custom scrollbar hiding classes (.hide-scroll) - html/body overflow behavioral rules **Component-Level CSS**: - `/src/styles/Article.css` - Article component styles - `/src/assets/heartAnimation.css` - Heart animation for likes (imported in LikedArticlesContext) - `/src/stories/*.css` - Storybook example component styles (button.css, header.css, page.css) **CSS Framework**: Tailwind CSS v4.0.3 with @tailwindcss/postcss and @tailwindcss/vite --- ### 3. CONTEXT/PROVIDER DETAILS **LikedArticlesContext** (`/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx`): - **Purpose**: Manages user's liked articles with persistence - **localStorage Key**: `"likedArticles"` - stores serialized array of WikiArticle objects - **Exports**: `useLikedArticles()` hook with shape: ```typescript { likedArticles: WikiArticle[], toggleLike: (article: WikiArticle) => void, isLiked: (pageid: number) => boolean } ``` - **Side Effects**: Renders animated heart on like (using heart-animation CSS class) - **Initialization**: Reads from localStorage on mount, defaults to empty array --- ### 4. DATA FETCHING HOOKS & API CALLS **useWikiArticles** (`/src/hooks/useWikiArticles.ts`): - **API Endpoint**: Dynamic per language from `currentLanguage.api` (Wikipedia API endpoints) - **Base URLs**: Multiple language-specific Wikipedia APIs (en, ar, bn, ca, de, es, etc. - 50+ languages) - **Fetch Pattern**: ``` GET /w/api.php?action=query&generator=random&prop=extracts|info|pageimages ``` - **Query Parameters**: - `action=query` - `generator=random` (random articles) - `grnnamespace=0` (mainspace only) - `prop=extracts|info|pageimages` (text, metadata, thumbnails) - `exintro=1` (intro only) - `exlimit=max` & `exsentences=5` (5 sentence extracts) - `explaintext=1` (plain text) - `piprop=thumbnail&pithumbsize=800` (thumbnails) - `origin=*` (CORS) - **Data Shape Returned**: ```typescript WikiArticle { title: string, displaytitle: string, extract: string, pageid: number, url: string, thumbnail: { source: string, width: number, height: number } } ``` - **Buffering**: Fetches in batches of 20 articles with preloading of images - **State**: `articles`, `loading`, `buffer` **useLocalization** (`/src/hooks/useLocalization.ts`): - **localStorage Key**: `"lang"` - stores language ID (default: English) - **Returns**: `currentLanguage` (Language object) and `setLanguage` function - **Side Effect**: `window.location.reload()` on language change - **Languages**: 50+ languages with Wikipedia API URLs and flag images --- ### 5. localStorage & SessionStorage KEYS Read during render/initialization: - `"likedArticles"` - Array of liked WikiArticle objects (LikedArticlesProvider) - `"lang"` - Language ID string (useLocalization, default: "en") --- ### 6. PORTAL ROOTS & SPECIAL DOM ELEMENTS **No createPortal usage detected in app code.** **Main Root Element**: `
` in index.html **No modal/dialog/toast portal roots** - The app uses inline conditional rendering (not React Portals) for: - About modal (inline in App.tsx) - Likes sidebar (inline in App.tsx) - Heart animation (renders from LikedArticlesProvider) --- ### 7. COMPLETE COMPONENT INVENTORY **Core App Components**: 1. `App` (`/src/App.tsx`) - Main entry point, manages UI state 2. `WikiCard` (`/src/components/WikiCard.tsx`) - Single article card (full-screen card UI) 3. `LanguageSelector` (`/src/components/LanguageSelector.tsx`) - Language dropdown 4. `ArticleList` (`/src/components/ArticleList.tsx`) - List wrapper for articles 5. `Article` (`/src/components/Article.tsx`) - Generic article component **Storybook Example Components**: 1. `Button` (`/src/stories/Button.tsx`) - Reusable button component 2. `Header` (`/src/stories/Header.tsx`) - Header with user state 3. `Page` (`/src/stories/Page.tsx`) - Full page example **Types**: - `WikiArticle` interface (in WikiCard.tsx) - `ArticleProps` type (`/src/types/ArticleProps.ts`) - `Language` configuration (in languages.ts) --- ### 8. STORYBOOK CONFIGURATION **Location**: `/.storybook/` **main.ts** (`/.storybook/main.ts`): ```typescript { stories: ['../src/**/*.mdx', '../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)', './eval-support/*.mdx'], addons: [ '@chromatic-com/storybook', '@storybook/addon-vitest', '@storybook/addon-a11y', '@storybook/addon-docs', '@storybook/addon-onboarding' ], framework: '@storybook/react-vite' } ``` **preview.tsx** (`/.storybook/preview.tsx`): ```typescript { parameters: { controls: { matchers: { color: /(background|color)$/i, date: /Date$/i } }, a11y: { test: 'todo' } } } ``` **Current Stories**: - Button (Primary, Secondary, Large, Small variants) - Header (LoggedIn, LoggedOut states) - Page (LoggedOut, LoggedIn with interaction testing) --- ### 9. PACKAGE.json DEPENDENCIES **Core React**: - react ^18.3.1 - react-dom ^18.3.1 **UI & Styling**: - tailwindcss ^4.0.3 - @tailwindcss/postcss ^4.0.3 - @tailwindcss/vite ^4.0.3 - lucide-react ^0.474.0 (icons) **Build & Dev**: - vite ^6.0.5 - @vitejs/plugin-react ^4.3.4 - typescript ~5.6.2 - vite-plugin-pwa ^0.21.1 (PWA support) **Storybook** (pre-release build): - storybook 0.0.0-pr-34421-sha-8babe7bc - @storybook/react-vite (same version) - @storybook/addon-vitest (same version) - @storybook/addon-a11y (same version) - @storybook/addon-docs (same version) - @storybook/addon-onboarding (same version) - @chromatic-com/storybook ^5.1.1 **Testing**: - vitest ^4.1.2 - @vitest/browser-playwright ^4.1.2 - @vitest/coverage-v8 ^4.1.2 - playwright ^1.58.2 **Analytics**: - @vercel/analytics ^1.4.1 --- ### 10. VITE CONFIGURATION DETAILS **vite.config.ts**: - React plugin enabled - Tailwind CSS plugin enabled - PWA plugin with auto-update registration - Vitest integration with Storybook addon-vitest - Playwright browser testing configured - Special Playwright config for mobile screenshots (iPhone 17 userAgent, 393x852 viewport, 2x scale) --- ### 11. KEY RUNTIME CHARACTERISTICS **Browser State Access**: - `navigator.share()` - for native share (WikiCard) - `navigator.clipboard` - for fallback link copying - `IntersectionObserver` - for infinite scroll pagination (App.tsx) - `localStorage` - for persistence (2 keys: lang, likedArticles) **Double-Click Handler**: - WikiCard responds to double-click to toggle like (like TikTok) **Image Preloading**: - All fetched article images preloaded before adding to display list **Analytics**: - Vercel Analytics component renders in App.tsx --- ### 12. EXISTING STORYBOOK STORIES & PATTERNS **Story Files**: - `/src/stories/Button.stories.ts` - Meta + 4 variants (Primary, Secondary, Large, Small) - `/src/stories/Header.stories.ts` - Meta + 2 states (LoggedIn, LoggedOut) - `/src/stories/Page.stories.ts` - Meta + 2 states + interaction test (play function) **Testing Pattern**: Using `expect`, `userEvent`, `within` from 'storybook/test' **Addon Configuration**: Vitest addon enabled for play function testing, A11y addon for accessibility tests --- ### CRITICAL SETUP NOTES FOR STORYBOOK 1. **Provider Wrapping**: Stories using `useLikedArticles` or `useLocalization` MUST wrap components with `LikedArticlesProvider` 2. **API Calls**: `useWikiArticles` makes real Wikipedia API calls - may want to mock in Storybook stories 3. **localStorage**: Both hooks read localStorage on mount - need to mock or reset between stories 4. **Window.reload**: `setLanguage` calls `window.location.reload()` - avoid in Storybook or mock 5. **No Portals**: App uses inline rendering, so no special portal setup needed 6. **Tailwind**: Global Tailwind v4 styles imported - should work in preview.tsx 7. **Mobile-First**: App is mobile-optimized with specific viewport handling in vite.config 8. **PWA Ready**: Service worker registration in place (handled by vite-plugin-pwa) --- **File Paths Summary**: - Entry: `/src/main.tsx` - App Root: `/src/App.tsx` - Providers: `/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx` - Hooks: `/src/hooks/useWikiArticles.ts`, `/src/hooks/useLocalization.ts` - Components: `/src/components/WikiCard.tsx`, `/src/components/LanguageSelector.tsx`, `/src/components/ArticleList.tsx`, `/src/components/Article.tsx` - Types: `/src/types/ArticleProps.ts` - Config: `/src/languages.ts` - CSS: `/src/index.css`, `/src/styles/Article.css`, `/src/assets/heartAnimation.css` - Storybook: `/.storybook/main.ts`, `/.storybook/preview.tsx`

content.text:
All 17 tests pass across 5 story files. Here's a summary of everything that was set up:

## Summary

### Preview setup (`.storybook/preview.tsx`)
- Imports `../src/index.css` (Tailwind v4 global styles)
- Wraps all stories with `LikedArticlesProvider` (the only app provider)
- Seeds `localStorage.setItem('lang', 'en')` before each story (read by `useLocalization`)
- Configures MSW with `initialize` + `mswLoader` for Wikipedia API mocking

### MSW setup
- Installed `msw` + `msw-storybook-addon`
- Ran `msw init ./public` to generate the service worker
- Added `staticDirs: ['../public']` to `main.ts`
- Created `.storybook/msw-handlers.ts` with a global Wikipedia API mock returning 3 realistic articles (Einstein, Curie, Everest)

### Stories written (17 tests total)

| File | Stories |
|------|---------|
| `src/components/Article.stories.tsx` | Default, WithImage, LongContent |
| `src/components/ArticleList.stories.tsx` | Default, Empty, WithSelectionHandler |
| `src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx` | Default, WithExtract, NoThumbnail, Liked |
| `src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx` | Default, DropdownOpen, DropdownShowsLanguages |
| `src/App.stories.tsx` | Default, AboutModalOpen, LikesModalOpen, WithArticlesLoaded |

Every story has a meaningful `play` function with assertions. The `App` stories exercise the full provider tree, MSW-mocked Wikipedia API, and modal interactions.
